sections.heading: Introduction
sections.content: In modern Android development, starting background work is not enough; you must also stop it when necessary. If a user leaves a screen while work is running, that task should be cancelled immediately. This is where Structured Concurrency and Cooperative Cancellation become essential.

sections.heading: 1. Structured Concurrency
sections.content: Structured Concurrency ensures that when a parent coroutine is cancelled, all of its children are cancelled automatically. You manage the Scope instead of manually tracking every task.

sections.heading: Key Concepts
items: Parent-Child Bond: If a child coroutine fails, it usually cancels its parent and siblings (unless using SupervisorJob).
Leak Prevention: Using viewModelScope or lifecycleScope ensures coroutines cancel when the UI is destroyed.

sections.heading: 2. Cancellation is Cooperative
sections.content: Coroutines do not stop instantly when cancelled. They must cooperate by checking their cancellation status periodically.

sections.heading: How to Check for Cancellation
items: Suspending Functions: delay(), withContext(), and most networking/database calls are cancellable.
isActive: Manually check inside long-running loops.
ensureActive(): Throws CancellationException if the coroutine is cancelled.
yield(): Suspends briefly and checks for cancellation.

sections.heading: Checking Cancellation in a Loop
sections.content: Use ensureActive() inside heavy loops to immediately stop execution if the coroutine is cancelled.

sections.heading: 3. Handling Cancellation (Cleanup)
sections.content: When a coroutine is cancelled, it throws CancellationException. Use try-finally to clean up resources. If you must call a suspending function during cleanup, wrap it with NonCancellable.

sections.heading: 4. Exception Handling with SupervisorJob
sections.content: By default, if one child coroutine fails, the entire hierarchy is cancelled. SupervisorJob prevents this behavior, allowing sibling coroutines to continue running.

sections.heading: 5. CoroutineExceptionHandler
sections.content: CoroutineExceptionHandler catches uncaught exceptions in a coroutine. It acts as a global catch for that specific coroutine.

sections.heading: Summary
items: Cancellation is cooperative and must be checked in your code.
Structured Concurrency ensures parent scopes control child lifetimes.
NonCancellable allows cleanup even after cancellation.
SupervisorJob prevents one failing coroutine from cancelling its siblings.
